what_it_does: This file provides a small synchronous file logger for the recollection package: it defines a LogLevel union, resolves a logs directory under the superpowers directory, builds a path to episodic-memory.log, formats timestamped log lines, and appends them to that file.
observations: Imports fs and path, plus getSuperpowersDir from ./paths.js.
getLogDir creates the logs directory recursively if it does not exist.
formatLogLine produces an ISO timestamped, newline-terminated string.
appendLogLine uses fs.appendFileSync to write the formatted line.
standouts: The log file is explicitly named episodic-memory.log, tying the logger to episodic memory semantics.
Every log append is a synchronous filesystem call.
The log directory is lazily auto-created on first access.
conventions: TypeScript with explicit return type annotations.
Uses path.join for cross-platform path construction.
Guards directory creation with fs.existsSync before fs.mkdirSync recursive.
ISO 8601 timestamp prefix and fixed [level] bracket format.
concerns: Synchronous appendFileSync can block the event loop under high log volume.
No log rotation, size limits, or retention policy.
No error handling if the append operation fails.
No concurrency guarantees if multiple processes write the same log file.
notes: A minimal, synchronous, line-oriented file logger that auto-creates its directory and writes to episodic-memory.log.
questions: What volume of log writes is expected, given the synchronous implementation?
Is there a deliberate reason to use sync I/O instead of async or a stream?
Are multiple processes expected to share this log file?
Is log retention or rotation handled elsewhere?
intention_signals: The module appears designed to give the recollection system a dependable, low-ceremony way to persist timestamped events to a local text log, favoring immediate durability over performance or advanced log management.
contribution: A compact, deterministic synchronous logging utility that establishes a dedicated log directory, formats timestamped lines, and appends them to a named episodic-memory.log file.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that writes timestamped info/warn/error log lines to a local episodic-memory.log file under the superpowers logs directory using synchronous filesystem operations.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the recollection package with a simple, durable, append-only text log for recording events.

symbols.does: Declares the allowed log level values.
symbols.description: LogLevel is a string union type restricting levels to 'info', 'warn', and 'error', used by formatting and append functions.

symbols.does: Returns and lazily creates the logs directory.
symbols.description: getLogDir resolves a 'logs' subdirectory under the superpowers directory and creates it recursively if it does not already exist, then returns the path.

symbols.does: Returns the full path to the episodic-memory.log file.
symbols.description: getSyncLogPath joins the log directory with the fixed filename 'episodic-memory.log' to produce the target file path.

symbols.does: Formats a log entry as a timestamped line.
symbols.description: formatLogLine takes a LogLevel and a message and returns an ISO 8601 timestamped, bracketed-level, newline-terminated string suitable for appending to a text log.

symbols.does: Appends a formatted log line to the sync log file.
symbols.description: appendLogLine formats the given level and message and synchronously appends the resulting line to the file returned by getSyncLogPath.
